en.extract:
The Palau flycatcher (Myiagra erythrops) is a species of bird in the family Monarchidae. It is endemic to Palau.


Taxonomy and systematics
Some authorities consider the Palau flycatcher to be a subspecies of the oceanic flycatcher. Alternate names include mangrove flycatcher, Micronesian broadbill, Palau broadbill, Palau Myiagra flycatcher, rufous-faced flycatcher and rufous-faced Myiagra flycatcher.


References


External links

The '''Palau flycatcher''' ('''''Myiagra erythrops''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Monarchidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Palau]].

==Taxonomy and systematics==
Some authorities consider the Palau flycatcher to be a subspecies of the [[oceanic flycatcher]]. Alternate names include '''mangrove flycatcher''', '''Micronesian broadbill''', '''Palau broadbill''', '''Palau Myiagra flycatcher''', '''rufous-faced flycatcher''' and '''rufous-faced Myiagra flycatcher'''.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?lang=EN&avibaseid=EE035E28&sec=summary&ssver=1|title=Myiagra erythrops - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2017-01-23}}</ref>
